accurate.why: All claims accurately reflect the bill text; agencies mentioned and deadlines are correct.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the purpose — creating the National Parkinson's Project to prevent, diagnose, treat, and cure Parkinson's.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Implicitly covers the new statutory section; does not explicitly name the Public Health Service Act but adequately describes what was established.

exceptions_conditions.why: Notes the 2035 sunset and key deadlines.

effective_dates.why: Correctly states 24-month deadline for first annual assessment, 18-month deadline for first Advisory Council report, and 2035 sunset.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers the National Parkinson's Project, Secretary's duties, Advisory Council structure and reporting, information sharing, and sunset.

concise.why: Quite detailed and lengthy, though less so than some other candidates; provides more granularity than needed for a summary.

correct_entities.why: NIH, CDC, VA and other agencies correctly named.

correct_figures.why: All deadlines and the 2035 sunset correctly stated.

judged_via: claude-sonnet-4-6 via parallel Claude Code subagents (web/knowledge-verified context)
